Data analytics — student resource guide

A subjective curated selection of resources to support your learning in data visualisation, statistics and R. Resources are tagged by topic and tool.

Online resources

R Graph Gallery
Yan Holtz
R Python Data Viz Stats
Excellent guide to the many different chart types, how to use them, the R code for them, and how to style and refine.
Now available for python also: python-graph-gallery.com
From Data to Viz
Yan Holtz
Data Viz Stats
Helps you decide which chart type suits your data — a great starting point and is tool agnostic.
The Compendium - where to start when you don’t know where to start
Selva Prabhakaran
R Data Viz Stats
Really useful guide, described as “Practical statistics and R, written for working data scientists. Curated reading paths to begin, the full archive to depth.”
Data Visualization & Information Design blog
Cedric Scherer
R Data Viz
Blog for excellent visualisation predominantly using ggplot2.
Open Visualization Academy
Alberto Cairo
R Data Viz Stats
A free repository of knowledge about information design, data visualisation, and data literacy. Some focus on R
StatQuest with Josh Starmer
Josh Starmer
R Python Stats
Highly recommended for building statistical foundations, teaches statistics and machine learning terminology and concepts. The video index can be found here.
Allison Horst data science illustrations
Allison Horst
R Stats
Excellent illustrations to help explain various R programming and statistical concepts.
Coding Club: Efficient and beautiful data synthesis. Taking your tidyverse skills to the next level.
Gergana
R Data Viz
Tutorials to augment your tidyverse skills.
Tidy Tuesday
R Community
R Python Data Viz
Weekly community challenge, their over-arching goal is to provide real-world datasets so that people can learn to work with data. Original goal was to have a responsive online space for learners and mentors to gather and work through R for Data Science by Hadley Wickham, Mine Çetinkaya-Rundel, and Garrett Grolemund. Expanded to include other programming languages such as python and Julia. Examples from 2021 were gathered into an interactive resource.
Royal Statistical Society - Training Courses
RSS
Stats PAID
High calibre CPD training courses available online. I recommend the Intermediate Statistics course as a follow on from the Professional Diploma course (if budget allows!).
Master Data visualization with ggplot2
Cedric Scherer & Yan Holtz
R Data Viz
Lessons developed by Cedric Scherer and Yan Hotlz for data Visualization in R. Some free content.

Posit specific resources

posit cheatsheets
posit
R Data Viz Stats
Quick reference guide for posit (formerly RStudio) tools and packages.
tidymodels - a framework for modelling data in R
Julia Silge and Max Kuhn (and many others)
R Stats
The tidymodels framework is a collection of packages for modeling and machine learning in R using tidyverse principles.
Creating tables in R using Great Tables package
posit
R Data Viz
The Great Tables package is all about making it simple to produce nice-looking display tables.

Books

Free online R books

R for Data Science (2nd ed.)
Hadley Wickham
R Data Viz Stats

The best place to start or refresh your R skills. Covers the tidyverse, ggplot2, data wrangling, and more.

Best R Books: A Curated Reading List That Won’t Waste Your Time
Selva Prabhakaran
R Data Viz Stats

A curated index linking to many excellent and up to date free online R books across many topics including:

  • the grammar of graphics/ visualisation
  • statistics
  • creating books and websites
  • developing R packages
  • text mining

Statistics

An Introduction to Statistical Learning
Gareth James et al
R Python Stats

Standard entry point for statistical learning and machine learning in R, does assume some statistics background but is written to be accessible. Note, also available for python.

The StatQuest Illustrated Guide to Machine Learning
Josh Starmer
Stats

Breaks down statistical and machine learning concepts and clearly explains them - check out his YouTube first to see if his approach suits your learning style.

The StatQuest Illustrated Guide to Statistics
Josh Starmer
R Python Stats

Breaks down statistical concepts and clearly explains them - check out his YouTube first to see if his approach suits your learning style.

Regression and Other Stories
Andrew Gelamn, Jennifer Hill and Aki Vehtari et al
R Stats

Focuses on applied regression and uses R throughout, very practical.

The Art of Statistics: Learning from Data
David Spiegelhalter
Stats

A very accessible book, written for a general audience, to helping you think like a statistician, showing how data can be used to answer questions, tell stories and avoid being misled.

Data visualisation

CHART: Designing Creative Data Visualizations from Charts to Art
Nadieh Bremer
Data Viz

A beautiful book breaking down the creative process of turning data into artistic data visualisations into lessons. Useful as both a practical reference and as inspiration for pushing beyond default chart types.

Speak Data
Giorgia Lupi and Phillip Cox
Data Viz

I haven’t read this yet but I have seen a talk based on it and it looks like a brilliant book. “In this beautifully illustrated book, the authors present data as a vocabulary that anyone can use, showing that when we truly learn to ‘speak data’, we can open up new worlds of meaning about ourselves and everything around us.”

Data Sketches
Nadieh Bremer & Shirley Wu
Data Viz

Another stunning book from Nadieh Bremer - this time in collaboration with Shirley Wu. Showcases 12 topics and how the 2 designers approached collecting the data, developing and producing the visualisations to produce 24 projects; an amazing insight to their creative processes.

Knowledge is Beautiful
David McCandless Wu
Data Viz

Interesting coffee table book of visualisations. Purely visual, not a how-to, a visual showcase instead. A little dated (printed 2014) but as a source of inspiration still very useful.

Making with Data: Physical Design and Craft in a Data-Driven World
Samual Huron et al Wu
Data Viz

Very niche! I don’t have this yet, have just looked at previews and have watched a seminar relating to it, centres around data physicalisation - how can we give data physical form.

Record, Map and Capture in Textile Art: Data visualization in cloth and stitch
Jordan Cunliffe Wu
Data Viz

Again, very niche! This is a beautiful book about “data physicalisation” - how different artists represent and visualise data through stitching and textiles


Data visualisation designers

Giorgia Lupi
Data humanist & designer

Known for her humanistic, handcrafted approach to data visualisation.

Sonja Kuijpers - Studio Terp
Data visualization artist

Creates evocative, artistic Data visualizations - wonderful for broadening ideas of what data viz can be.

Compiled for student use by Dr Philippa Wilkes. Resources reflect personal opinion only; the author has no affiliation with any of the resources or individuals mentioned. Last updated: May 2026.
